response.justification: Sarah Smith is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js expertise, good software craftsmanship habits, and some interesting developer tooling work, but she is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. There is no demonstrated Java or Kotlin depth, no JVM internals experience, and no exposure to Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate anywhere in the resume. Java appears once in the skills list and twice in passing job descriptions, but never as a primary language. The NI LabWindows role is the closest IDE-adjacent experience, but it was C/C++ and lasted only six months. The role demands deep JVM and enterprise framework expertise as table-stakes qualifications that are simply absent here.
strengths: Consistent software craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) across multiple roles
Genuine developer tooling experience: built open-source test runners, assertion libraries, and MCP code generators
Brief but real IDE internals exposure from NI LabWindows C IDE component work
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin experience; primary language stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework usage
No JVM internals knowledge demonstrated — bytecode, classloading, GC tuning, or related tooling are entirely absent

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework exposure
Developer tooling and open-source library authorship
